programme.desc: Out-of-work journalist Fred Flarsky secures employment writing speeches for glamorous US Secretary of State Charlotte Field, who is shortly to run for office as the first female president. Opposites attract and a very unlikely romance develops during an international campaign to champion environmental issues. Comedy drama, starring Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville hears the bewildering sales tactics of cold callers pursuing the vulnerable. Plus, what happens when a car insurer seems to believe a vehicle is not worth fixing

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress on a Cabus bungalow, a three-storey house in Newport, and two lots by the side of a canal and river in West Stockwith

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley tells the true stories of the con woman who stole £1million from the public purse to fund her lavish lifestyle and the trusted parish clerk who secretly transferred the entire council budget to himself

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Rescue workers battle to save two students on Dartmoor when their island camp is cut off by a flooded river. A shop owner and his customers have a lucky escape when a car crashes through the window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells and learns all about a traditional Welsh love token

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al unwillingly finds himself on a blind date but makes an unexpected connection with a woman who may not be everything she seems

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Ryan, Sherina, Richard and Anna

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a house hunting mission in the Yorkshire Dales with a pair of brothers, one of whom wants his £500,000 budget to furnish him with a country home in rural isolation. While in the area, Nicki joins the Dales Park Rangers and helps them on their everyday rounds as well, as learning about the growing popularity of geocaching with walkers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore tries to make money out of a small set of drawers, a gentleman's wardrobe and four lawn chairs, with the help of print artist Zoe Murphy and designer Lucy Tiffney

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper finds out about British workhouses and Arusha Irvine learns the history of Victorian toilets as the pair continue their antiques search across Dundee and Yorkshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Jo Scott lends a hand at Crom Estate in Co Fermanagh with the annual crossing of the swimming cattle, and Gavin Andrews hits the trails in Castlewellan with the mountain bikers giving back by repairing trails. Wildlife expert Conor McKinney meets a couple who have transformed a once barren landscape into a wildlife refuge, and Jo is behind the scenes at Streamvale Open Farm as it prepares to reopen to the public

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher takes on an antique toy cannon handed down to the owner by his grandfather, who bought it with his pocked money in 1907. It hasn't fired a shot for more than 50 years. Can Steve get it ready for combat once again? Instrument restorer Julyan Wallis takes on an 80-year-old banjo, Suzie Fletcher gets to work on a pair of 1930s roller skates and woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to bring an elaborate wooden salad bowl back to life

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's children's week in the sewing room and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young's pattern challenge choice is a classic for any toddler's wardrobe - a romper suit. The transformation task sees the sewers turning an adult wetsuit into a kids' fancy dress costume, before they tackle a children's raincoat, remembering it must be fully waterproof but allow enough movement for jumping in muddy puddles

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look at the biggest stories of this season's FA Cup ahead of this weekend's final between Chelsea and Leicester City at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Amy wants to know if the reason ex Marney never made them official was because he was actually cheating on her. Lucy wants to find out why ex Ethan was sliding into her mates DMs when they were together. Steve thought he had found love in Amira, until she ghosted him and moved on. Now reunited for the first time, Amira reveals the shocking truth that could end them for good

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford hear about the damage caused by delivery drivers that's left homeowners with broken walls and big bills - after the vehicles involved simply drove off. Plus, the innocent victims caught up in a shocking new wave of identity fraud that has swept the country during the pandemic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin heads to his old stomping ground, near Stratford-upon-Avon, to see an intriguing lot consisting of 16 garages. Tommy Walsh is in Wolverhampton looking at a three-bed detached property, and Martel Martel is in Sunderland where a four bed mid-terrace has a bit of a damp problem

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on the dedicated fraud-fighters pursuing the criminals who steal £40billion a year from the public purse. This edition features a supposedly trustworthy local pharmacist 'doctoring' prescriptions to earn £76,000 and the corrupt council workers taking £1million of public money in a sophisticated inside job

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Family members recount how they had to flee their home when a battery charger, bought online, started a fire that destroyed their house. Plus, footage of a paddle boarder and a drone operator combining their skills to save four horses cut off after a river burst its banks. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper guide two teams scouring Nottingham racecourse for bargains to take to auction. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid needs help to come up with a final Labour of Hercules to raise money, and Emma tries to organise a Sunday lunch with Luca. Daniel gets embroiled in a prison dispute and has to decide how to deal with information he shouldn't have

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers teams up with upcycler Sarah Peterson and lighting expert Duncan McKean to revive a pair of side tables, a rattan chair and some vintage lawnmower buckets

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine finds herself with home advantage as she's joined by David Harper on the west coast of Scotland for the latest leg of their trip. After Roo's head gets stuck in an antique she decides to stick with what she knows best - old glass. David examines a rare personal collection that tells the story of a once booming industry all antiques fans should know about

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vi tells Stuart she's going to stay in Walford but he doesn't seem pleased at the thought. The doctor tells Bernadette she could face complications with a potential pregnancy, leaving Rainie unsure whether to proceed with the surrogacy. Kheerat and Sharon go out for a drink, where they discuss their romantic pasts - only for Kheerat to put his foot in it, leaving Sharon hurt

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A construction worker hopes his easy-assemble lighting system will spark some interest amid millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two ex-barmen think their nifty premium whisky subscription business will put them in with a shot of a cash investment, while a construction worker pitches his easy-assemble lighting system. An Isle of Man entrepreneur demonstrates her innovative sand removing powder and the Dragons are intrigued by a quirky nose-mounted air filter. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop & Power
programme.desc: Little Mix star Leigh-Anne Pinnock recounts her experiences of racism and colourism as a black woman in the UK, as well as looking at the wider race issues the country is facing. The girl band member compares experiences with Alexandra Burke, Nao, RAYE and Keisha Buchanan from the Sugababes, who each reveal their own shocking experiences within the music industry because of the colour of their skin. Leigh-Anne finds the more she learns about systemic racism, the more she feels she must do something

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers reviews the week's political news, inviting comment and analysis from Stormont and Westminster

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring all the goals, analysis of the talking points and post-match reaction from managers and players. All of the day's games will be covered, with the panel of former professionals analysing the tactics employed by the teams, as well as highlighting any deficiencies that led to a team's downfall

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford report on the online electronics retailer that has left dozens of customers in the lurch. Plus, Julia hears from the learners in lockdown who found self-education more expensive than they'd expected, explaining how to avoid falling foul of the small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of terraced houses in Carlisle and Abbey Wood in London, as well as a seven-year project to renovate a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on the dedicated fraud-fighters pursuing the criminals who steal £40billion a year from the public purse. This edition features the amateur actress who put on a performance to evade justice for a showstopping £750,000 fraud and the school business manager who embezzled over £200,000

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: How a mountain rescue team fight its way through appalling conditions to rescue a walker on Snowdon. Plus, the mountain biker who crash-landed after a jump, leading his friends to believe he's broken his neck. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross hits the fair to see if anyone can help him with a mystery object and later in the show the team heads to Derby for the highs and lows of the auction

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Billie, Seb, Victoria and Joshua vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Manchester, the buyers want to celebrate the day their lives changed for ever -10 years ago when they won the National Lottery. Will it be a molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist or a self-taught metal sculptor who fulfils their brief?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits Norfolk to help a father and daughter who hope their £700,000 budget will provide them with a large equestrian property and annexe to house their family of four, along with their horse. Nicki also heads to the Norfolk Broads and learns about the restoration efforts of the Norfolk Wherry Trust before taking to the water to sail one of these traditional wooden boats

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre, salvaging three unwanted gems so they can shine again. A broken display cabinet finds its way to furniture artist Chloe Kempster, designer Daniel Heath needs to set a 1970s dressing table back on the right path, and Sarah tries to help a chandelier gleam again

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: For their last outing, Arusha Irvine and David Harper enjoy a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other - and the elements - in a sporty classic car. David's hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the final showdown Roo gambles on a Georgian long case clock. The duo then head to North Shields for the auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen snaps when offered a free meal from the cafe, sick of everyone judging her and her family. Nancy reluctantly agrees to babysit for Sharon, but while looking after Albie she feels unwell and only just manages to put the child back in his playpen before collapsing to the ground with an epileptic seizure

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is over the moon that Kathy has accepted his proposal. Mandy awaits her trial at the magistrates court. Graham tells Kathy he has decided to leave the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tries to change Graham's mind about leaving. Stella invites Bernice and Gavin for tea at Home Farm. Butch sees Emily's dad hitting her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ruby is attempting to win Blaketons heart, and Nick's colleagues are enjoying teasing him over his mum's potential romance with the sergeant. An illiterate man, Eddie, is outraged when a cottage to rent was promised to him by his aunt, but instead ends up being sold. His girlfriend calls off their wedding because they don't have a house! There is also an investigation of a peeping Tom in the village. Auntie Eileen comes to visit, and Ruby sees her chance to return to London

programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an unpopular pub landlord is beaten to death, a police informant casts suspicion on his ex-wife and her new husband. It seems like an open-and-shut case - but Lane badly mishandles the entire investigation, forcing Wycliffe and Kersey to intervene. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Buried resentments rise to the surface when a German arrives in Aidensfield, and an elderly couple have a wedding anniversary to remember

programme.desc: Butch is suspicious when Emily does not turn up for work. Graham tells Kathy about his first wife's death. Eric urges Stella to turn Home Farm into a health and beauty spa

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch visits Emily at home and sees she has a badly bruised cheek. Ashley encourages Graham to make a new start. Roy and Kelly go house-hunting

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice is upset by the way Les takes her for granted. The doctors tell Liz and Steve that Jim may never walk again. Ashley feels guilty at the thought of leaving Zoe alone

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary visits Jim and tells him about his fall. Zoe promises to make amends over her behaviour. Des is suspicious of Samantha's motives

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The collapse of a seemingly airtight prosecution case reflects badly on the detective, and it is not long before the media are baying for his blood. To make matters worse, a previous investigation comes back to haunt him, and Lane gets caught in the crossfire. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: In the summer of 1967, Morse and Thursday are both devastated by Joan's abrupt departure from Oxford. The young detective responds to his loss by throwing himself into his work, investigating the suspicious drowning of a scientist who was part of a research team working on an early computer. At first it appears to be a simple suicide - until two more bodies are found in the river

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the fatal stabbing of a psychiatrist with a caseload of violent adolescent patients, Ronnie and Joe's first instinct is to question those he was treating. However, the case takes a disturbing turn when the detectives discover a hidden sleeping compartment in the victim's office which has clearly been used recently by one of his underage visitors - and evidence comes to light suggesting he may have been having an affair with one of them. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Ramon Tikaram and Hattie Morahan

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: Alice and Rob's relationship continues to disintegrate as evidence under questioning incriminates them further and the children grow closer to David. Meanwhile, Tom's attempts to save his marriage are destroyed by his subsequent arrest, and forensic evidence throws new light on David's original conviction

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The gruesome double murder of a building society manager and his wife leads to Wycliffe clashing with his superiors when he decides, against their orders, to investigate a licensed gun dealer who apparently had links to organised crime when living in London. Detective drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham, with Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan warns Stella against working with Eric. Butch is determined to do whatever it takes to clear his name. Frankie is mean to Zoe because of the pay cut

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine feels she has blown her chances with Greg. Fred urges Ashley to finish with Zoe. The doctors break the news to Jim that he may spend the rest of his life in a wheelchair

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve wants to pay for a full-time nurse for Jim. Nick explains to Ashley about his plan to get to Darren. Moira is furious when Les makes amorous approaches towards her

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A prostitute's client confesses to the murder of another working girl before vanishing into the night, leaving Wycliffe with the tricky task of tracking him down before he kills again. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While working with the drug squad, Ronnie and Joe find a skeleton that has been hidden inside the boot of a car in the River Thames. It turns out to be the remains of Taylor Kane, an undercover policeman who went missing in the 1980s - around the time of the Brixton riots. Through clever detection, unsettling evidence is unearthed and the team knows it must raise issues the police would rather forget. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, and featuring one of the last performances by Roger Lloyd-Pack, who died in January

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: David's exoneration allows him to regain custody of his children and intensifies the investigation into Rob, Alice and Tom, leading to a critical arrest. But as damaging accusations concerning DI Hudson's relationship with David threaten to derail the case, her determination to deliver justice finally begins to unravel the mystery of who killed Tara. Drama, starring Lee Ingleby, Hermione Norris, Daniel Ryan and Angel Coulby

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe and Frankie get ready for their trip. Zak and Lisa return to the village, and he has it out with Angie. Kathy tells Laura that she feels more than she should for Graham

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Les is breathalysed and taken to the police station. Steve accuses Liz of trying to use Jim's accident to get back together with him. Nick tries to visit Darren in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha tells Des she is pregnant with his baby. Maxine blackmails Les into buying drinks for her. Jim panics when he suffers chest pains and cannot breathe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A climber plunges to his death from a cliff face and a police informant claims it was no accident. A re-enactment of the incident adds fuel to the fire, and Wycliffe braces himself to interview the dead man's best friend. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Lynn Farleigh, Christian Solimeno and Paul Venables

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert struggles to come up with the money to repay loan sharks Alastair and Norman Tonken, who gave him £1,000 to clear his debts. Eleanor falls ill and Martin diagnoses a strangulated hernia, but when the ambulance goes to the wrong village, he has no alternative but to perform the emergency operation himself in his surgery. With Martin Clunes, Ian McNeice and Louise Jameson

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The hunt for the killer of a seemingly innocent family man brings Ronnie into contact with his old boss, former DI Natalie Chandler, whose father is accused of the crime. She wastes no time in persuading the detective to go above and beyond the call of duty to exonerate her dad and find the real culprit - even if that means getting him into trouble with an increasingly frustrated Wes. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Harriet Walter, Martin Jarvis and Roy Hudd

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When the body of an elderly woman is discovered at a well-known suicide spot, Ronnie soon detects foul play, with suspicion falling on the victim's son and daughter-in-law, as well as a doctor who has recently arrived in the UK. However, even after a confession is secured, Joe remains baffled as to the motive, and Kate struggles to remain professional - jeopardising her career and leaving Jake to prosecute the case alone. Guest starring Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.desc: Zoe jumps into the canal with Morgan when the police attempt to reach out to her. Gary and Nick dive in to try to save them. Liz is delighted when Jim comes out of his coma

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz remains at Jim's side as he attempts to communicate with her. Maxine fears that Fiona will sack her. Ashley feels Zoe would be better off without him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is over the moon that Kathy has accepted his proposal. Mandy awaits her trial at the magistrates court. Graham tells Kathy he has decided to leave the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy tries to change Graham's mind about leaving. Stella invites Bernice and Gavin for tea at Home Farm. Butch sees Emily's dad hitting her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ruby is attempting to win Blaketons heart, and Nick's colleagues are enjoying teasing him over his mum's potential romance with the sergeant. An illiterate man, Eddie, is outraged when a cottage to rent was promised to him by his aunt, but instead ends up being sold. His girlfriend calls off their wedding because they don't have a house! There is also an investigation of a peeping Tom in the village. Auntie Eileen comes to visit, and Ruby sees her chance to return to London

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A faulty TV aerial prompts George to borrow his neighbour's ladder - but relations become strained when the henpecked husband falls off the roof and starts pointing the finger of blame. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an unpopular pub landlord is beaten to death, a police informant casts suspicion on his ex-wife and her new husband. It seems like an open-and-shut case - but Lane badly mishandles the entire investigation, forcing Wycliffe and Kersey to intervene. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Buried resentments rise to the surface when a German arrives in Aidensfield, and an elderly couple have a wedding anniversary to remember

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch is suspicious when Emily does not turn up for work. Graham tells Kathy about his first wife's death. Eric urges Stella to turn Home Farm into a health and beauty spa

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Butch visits Emily at home and sees she has a badly bruised cheek. Ashley encourages Graham to make a new start. Roy and Kelly go house-hunting

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Janice is upset by the way Les takes her for granted. The doctors tell Liz and Steve that Jim may never walk again. Ashley feels guilty at the thought of leaving Zoe alone

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary visits Jim and tells him about his fall. Zoe promises to make amends over her behaviour. Des is suspicious of Samantha's motives

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The collapse of a seemingly airtight prosecution case reflects badly on the detective, and it is not long before the media are baying for his blood. To make matters worse, a previous investigation comes back to haunt him, and Lane gets caught in the crossfire. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: In the summer of 1967, Morse and Thursday are both devastated by Joan's abrupt departure from Oxford. The young detective responds to his loss by throwing himself into his work, investigating the suspicious drowning of a scientist who was part of a research team working on an early computer. At first it appears to be a simple suicide - until two more bodies are found in the river

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While investigating the fatal stabbing of a psychiatrist with a caseload of violent adolescent patients, Ronnie and Joe's first instinct is to question those he was treating. However, the case takes a disturbing turn when the detectives discover a hidden sleeping compartment in the victim's office which has clearly been used recently by one of his underage visitors - and evidence comes to light suggesting he may have been having an affair with one of them. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Ramon Tikaram and Hattie Morahan

programme.title: Innocent
programme.desc: Alice and Rob's relationship continues to disintegrate as evidence under questioning incriminates them further and the children grow closer to David. Meanwhile, Tom's attempts to save his marriage are destroyed by his subsequent arrest, and forensic evidence throws new light on David's original conviction

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The gruesome double murder of a building society manager and his wife leads to Wycliffe clashing with his superiors when he decides, against their orders, to investigate a licensed gun dealer who apparently had links to organised crime when living in London. Detective drama, guest starring Leslie Grantham, with Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.desc: Romance smiles on Ida after a chance meeting on her way to Jersey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: While working with the drug squad, Ronnie and Joe find a skeleton that has been hidden inside the boot of a car in the River Thames. It turns out to be the remains of Taylor Kane, an undercover policeman who went missing in the 1980s - around the time of the Brixton riots. Through clever detection, unsettling evidence is unearthed and the team knows it must raise issues the police would rather forget. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, and featuring one of the last performances by Roger Lloyd-Pack, who died in January

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A selection of the best World Cup Goals through the years - beginning with Geoff Hurst's hat-trick for England in 1966, right up to the 2018 World Cup in Russia

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The super-powered Nemesis agents investigate why a rogue nation is breaking an international treaty by testing nuclear weapons under Antarctica. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Strange, seemingly malevolent encoded messages sour a wealthy man's wedded bliss, prompting him to seek Holmes's expertise. Victorian mystery, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey comes up against a man who is his equal in all respects. The only difference is that he's on the wrong side of the law. Obsessed with capturing him, Dempsey tests Makepeace's patience to the limit. Will Dempsey succeed against impossible odds?

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the death of a burglar who was killed after seeing classified information about a foreign country, and resort to theft in a bid to uncover the embassy's sinister secrets. Liz Fraser and Michael Elphick guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry's girlfriend Debbie starts work as a mobile hairdresser. Unfortunately the first house she attends is robbed, and she and Terry become suspects. When Arthur becomes involved, the police take over his lock-up. Vintage comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The crusading heroes investigate a murder, which leads them to uncover ageing Nazis who are being held in a disused mine. Starring William Gaunt and Alexandra Bastedo

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Former boxer Tony Bellew and his coach Dave Coldwell join team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey on the fishing game show, to take part in a series of challenges

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Francesca Cumani and Ed Chamberlin present the opening day of the York Dante meeting, where the 2.40 Duke of York Stakes is the feature race. Plus, National Hunt coverage from Newton Abbot. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Oli Bell, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood delves into the ITV Sport archives to take a look back at James Hunt, Barry Sheene and a spectacular crash by Kelvin Burt

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A trip to Malta sees Harry called on to investigate the abduction of a woman's pet poodle. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is pleased when villainous old friend Jack ''Oily' Wragg returns to England, However, his pleasure melts away when he learns that there are old scores to settle and that Wragg intends to stage one final heist. Light-hearted crime drama, guest starring Pete Postlethwaite, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Jimmy Bullard and Jamie O'Hara join team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey at Anglers Paradise in Devon, trying to catch ornamental koi carp, blue orfe and golden tench

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Radio 2 DJ Jo Whiley, Benidorm barman Jake Canuso, Law & Order: UK's Georgia Taylor and comedian Marcus Brigstocke compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh asks the questions

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade turns his attention from freshwater mysteries to the world's oceans, going in search of the real creature behind the enduring sea serpent legend. Starting in his own backyard, Jeremy fishes in the English Channel on the trail of a violent attack. His investigation leads him to Thailand where the mythology of the sea serpent reveals a vital clue, before he heads to the unlikely location of France where he tries to finally solve the mystery and have a sea serpent encounter of his own

programme.title: Pale Rider (1985)
programme.desc: An unscrupulous landowner threatens to throw gold-miners off their land and take the riches for himself. As the law turns a blind eye to their troubles, the miners desperately need a saviour - who happens to arrive in the form of a mysterious preacher with formidable gunfighting skills. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, alongside Michael Moriarty, Carrie Snodgress and Richard Dysart

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Pale Rider (1985)
programme.desc: An unscrupulous landowner threatens to throw gold-miners off their land and take the riches for himself. As the law turns a blind eye to their troubles, the miners desperately need a saviour - who happens to arrive in the form of a mysterious preacher with formidable gunfighting skills. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, alongside Michael Moriarty, Carrie Snodgress and Richard Dysart

programme.title: Executive Decision (1996)
programme.desc: Terrorists armed with the world's most lethal nerve gas take over an airliner and demand a massive ransom - and only a crack team of intelligence experts can stop them before they carry out their threat to destroy Washington, DC. Action thriller, starring Kurt Russell, Steven Seagal, Halle Berry, Oliver Platt, David Suchet and John Leguizamo

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a biochemist-turned-distiller is making Britain's first commercially produced rum, and what it takes to make a George Cross - Britain's highest civilian award for bravery. Plus, the man who is going back to nature thanks to his handmade wooden canoes, and the Sheffield firm making the tools of the trade for the world finest furniture makers

programme.title: The Krays (1990)
programme.desc: Biopic of the infamous twin brothers who built up a criminal empire in the East End of London during the 1960s. Seizing territory from an already established rival by force, they quickly rose to prominence in the city's gangland culture. However, over time the brothers began to clash, while enemies stood ready to bring them down. Starring Gary and Martin Kemp, Billie Whitelaw and Tom Bell

programme.title: Passenger 57 (1992)
programme.desc: After his wife is shot dead during a robbery, a cop hands in his badge and takes up a job offer from an old friend to be a security adviser for a major transatlantic airline. On the flight taking him to his new employers in Los Angeles is an international terrorist being escorted to trial by the FBI - while the prisoner's accomplices are disguised as passengers and crew and plan to hijack the plane. Action thriller, with Wesley Snipes, Bruce Payne, Tom Sizemore, Alex Datcher, Bruce Greenwood and Elizabeth Hurley

Night two sees Kiri and Dale host a soiree that includes an introduction to the host's love of taxidermy. programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in south London. Elliot and James want to buy their first place together but are struggling to pin down exactly where. Their favourite areas are Putney and Richmond, but their money does not go as far as they would like. Pam and Ross are in Wimbledon, where former tennis professional Ross works. However, having been stung after the 2009 housing crisis, they are understandably cautious

programme.title: Davina McCall: Sex, Myths and the Menopause
programme.desc: Davina McCall was 44 when she began to experience hot flushes, depression and confusion - symptoms she hid until she discovered her body was starting the transition to menopause. Here she describes her own experience to bust the myths surrounding the menopause, tackling the mid-life taboos that can destroy women's jobs and relationships, and exposing the lack of specialist education for GPs and the confusion that still exists around hormone replacement therapy

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: The comedian's attempts to create a glamping site made up of repurposed old vehicles hit more problems when master craftsman Pauleo tells him his 1950s Maltese bus is not worth saving. Johnny buys another vintage camper van and decides this one is a surprise gift for Bev - now all he has to do is keep the secret from her. Meanwhile, the pair head to Yorkshire to check out a possible location for the glampsite, only for the rise of Covid-19 to threaten the entire enterprise

programme.title: Massacre at Ballymurphy
programme.desc: Documentary investigating the massacre in West Belfast in August 1971, in which 11 people died when British troops opened fire on Catholic residents. The film presents evidence suggesting that, contrary to the claims of the army at the time, all of the victims were innocent and unarmed. The families of the dead accuse Britain's elite Parachute Regiment of carrying out the shootings, the same regiment which, less than six months later, was to shoot dead 13 unarmed people on what became known the world over as Bloody Sunday

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to Sarah Kendall, Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack and Mike Wozniak. Sarah still leads the race to win Greg's stupendous skull-trophy, but the chasing pack are closing in, hoping beyond hope to score points by creatively munching on snacks, drawing pretty wings and being brutally honest about emergency plane landings. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Helen Lawal heads to one of the world's biggest coffee factories to find out how instant cappuccinos get their froth. Matt Tebbutt is in Naples to discover why most tinned tomatoes come from Italy, while Kate Quilton embarks on a panettone odyssey across Europe to learn how the fluffy cake lasts so long

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
programme.desc: The experts focus on wild gardens that celebrate nature. Poppy Okotcha creates a wild planting design that requires little maintenance, Joel Bird demonstrates how to make a living tipi, and Bruce Kenneth builds a table with its own herb patch and built-in bar. The team also visits a family in the Pennine hills looking for an outside space that goes with their view of the Saddleworth countryside

programme.title: Jabbed! Inside Britain's Vaccine Triumph
programme.desc: Documentary about the team responsible for sourcing Covid-19 vaccines for the UK Government, whose efforts were one of the biggest public health gambles in British history. Members of the team share their experiences of the critical six-month period from May to December in 2020, where millions of pounds were spent on vaccines before anyone knew for certain if they actually worked

programme.title: The £100k Drop
programme.desc: Couples from Fife and London play the quiz in which contestants lose some, or all, of the prize money with each wrong answer. Presented by Davina McCall

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists enjoy flyboarding on the beach near Santander, before aerial fun on a treetop assault course. But one of the contestant's flirting leads to tension at the vote

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the long-running words-and-numbers game, as food critic Jay Rayner joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Having been thrown out by his new wife Loretta, Carla's ex-husband Nick comes grovelling back to the bar, promising to devote himself to her if she will take him back. Comedy, starring Rhea Perlman and Dan Hedaya

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm's boss makes him an offer he can't refuse - accept a promotion or lose his job. Norm therefore finds himself working as a 'corporate killer' - the person in charge of firing other employees. Bar-room comedy, starring George Wendt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray worries Debra's criticism will make the children think he is a fool, and hatches a plan to win back their respect

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray refuses to take Debra to an awards ceremony, tired of her constant lateness, while Frank and Robert get into their own heated debate about the importance of punctuality. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton, Peter Boyle and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra wants to buy an expensive dress for Ally to wear to a friend's 13th birthday party, but Ray refuses to spend $250 on something that will only be used once, and naturally Marie takes his side. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Madylin Sweeten

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink arranges a date for Daphne with his new radio station manager - unaware he is gay. Comedy, guest starring Eric Lutes, with Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles tries to prepare himself for the demands of fatherhood - but soon realises that carrying a 10lb bag of flour around the city streets is no substitute for a real baby. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier attend the closing of Martin's favourite bar, but are shocked to discover it is being demolished by a company they have invested in. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie gets green-fingered as she sets out to restore the chateau's original greenhouse, which has recently been damaged in a storm. Lee and Belinda continue to expand their new outdoor venue. When their first wedding falls through, they fortunately get a new booking for a 60th birthday, but then end up in a race against time to get the garden party ready. With 12 acres of land to enjoy, Dick and Angel make sure their Wellington boots have a home, creating bootjacks for the newly renovated boot room

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, married couple Karen and DJ Paul are first to host with their Ibiza-themed evening. They are joined by outdoorsy Lucy and her partner Paul, who is left hungry in every course. The third couple are Zimbabwean social worker Pat and her husband Tino who are blown away by the entertainment - but not so much by the food. On day two, Pat and Tino are determined to deliver a winning Zimbabwean evening with big portions to satisfy Paul. But their unconventional dessert of mashed sweet potato proves a little too adventurous for their guests. Finally, it's Lucy and Paul's chance to show off their outdoorsy grub but their menu involves a lot of cooking on the night, leaving them stressed out while their guests are waiting

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with Richard Madeley joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: The Answer Trap
programme.desc: Pembrokeshire couple Nikola and Lee take on siblings Kelly and Matt from Ipswich, sorting a series of answers into the correct categories to be in with a chance of winning a cash prize. However, hiding in each list of answers are wickedly deceptive and entirely plausible 'Answer Traps'. Hosted by Anita Rani

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Steve and Aniko want to escape the rat race and move to the peace and quiet of the French countryside in west Brittany. Steve is a music composer and Aniko a singer, so their dream would be to be able to create their own recording studio at home. With a budget of £90,000, property expert Jonnie Irwin lines up five options that he hopes could be just the ticket for the pair

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Villa Farm Cottage in Thornton Curtis in Lincolnshire, where perfectionist Petra Coates and her partner Martin Littlefield run a tight ship. When the guests arrive, the bathroom tiles take Andrew back to his public swimming pool days and Mark has a moan about a missing en suite. In the morning, the guests are spoilt for choice as Petra provides a German cold meat feast followed by an English breakfast cooked by Martin. However, Andrew and Qidi's feedback about the tiling leaves Martin and Petra planning to hit back on payment day

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Inspired by his South American travels, amateur cook John Paul hopes that his spicy pork tacos will be a tough tackle for chef Sophie Michell as he cooks for the cash. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Martine confides in Grace as she awaits her biopsy results. Maxine decides she is washing her hands with Warren after discovering some shocking news

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A Liverpool family face a difficult decision choosing which one of a litter of 10 German shepherd puppies to take home with them. The Wood Green staff also hope a Staffordshire bull terrier, brought in by social services after an incident in its previous home, will be able to find a more understanding owner, and a Yorkshire terrier with a big attitude tries to win over a couple

programme.title: Taskmaster

Lee has a makeover, Jamali gets destructive with some rubble, and Mike warns Greg's assistant Alex Horne about some potentially dangerous dairy produce. Meanwhile, Sarah is still the one to catch

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: Frank doesn't react well when he learns that Mary is planning to sell their home, meaning he'll finally have to move out. The second she leaves the house, he locks her out and claims ownership, barricading himself in - along with Aine, Doofus, Nicola and Stephane - and refusing to give up his territory. However, with Mary equally determined to get back in the property, the two face-off in a battle of wills. Comedy, starring Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: Frank's birthday sees Doofus, Mary and Aine willing to gift him an intervention - his life's stuck in a cycle, and they want him to break free. Their bid coincides with the return of one of Mary's old flames, Liam - a self-described 'wild rover' who's never really grown up. His arrival gives Frank the chance to see what his life will be like if he doesn't turn things around. Brendan Gleeson guest stars, with Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on programmes including Line of Duty, I Can See Your Voice, Beat the Chasers, Sex and the City, My Octopus Teacher, Catfish UK: The TV Show and The Earth at Night in Color. Plus, they offer comment on ITV's coverage of the apparent ease of access to the Prime Minister's personal telephone number

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Notting Hill, West London, Queen and Shawn kick off the culinary competition with a menu inspired by their Thai and West Indian heritage, but well-travelled foodies Suzy and Paul are unimpressed. Eve Humphreys provides some low-cost DIY tips, while Gary Usher shows them how to make their favourite takeaway at home for a fraction of the cost

His cynical outlook begins to change when he falls for a single mother working toward her degree, while a talented pupil makes him consider what he wants from his life. Romantic comedy, with Hugh Grant, Marisa Tomei, Allison Janney and JK Simmons

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: With debate raging over how some members of the royal family earn a private income, Dispatches goes undercover to find out what some of the Queen's family might be prepared to do for money. programme.title: Cheers

programme.desc: Robert contemplates leaving the police force to take up a job selling security systems, but the proposal receives a mixed reaction, leading to tense celebrations at his birthday party. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Brad Garrett and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is delighted to be awarded an honorary doctorate, but hurts Debra's feelings by thanking everyone except her in his acceptance speech. He claims it was an accident, though she takes the opportunity to remind him of past snubs that were obviously deliberate

With a budget of £135,000, they want an apartment or town house close to the beach around La Cala de Mijas, with two bedrooms so their family can come to visit. Danni Menzies rises to the challenge to find them the perfect property

Against the odds, however, the opponents end up falling for one another

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: A report from Yangon, the largest city in Myanmar, as young protestors risk everything to defy the military junta's coup. As the death toll in the city mounts, cameras witness the early optimism and hope of a protest movement disintegrate as the security services launch a brutal crackdown on unarmed civilians. The Myanmar government says it is responding to protests that harm the stability of the nation, using non-lethal force. However, as pressure to release political prisoners and journalists intensifies, so does the bloody crackdown

programme.desc: Fourth series of the sitcom, starring Paul Ritter and Tamsin Greig. Dad develops a serious dislike of an annoying man with a bad habit of talking with his eyes closed. However, when he accidentally invites him to come round for dinner, he has a hard time getting rid of him

programme.desc: The boys face going without crumble if they refuse to help assemble a wooden shelf for Mum's old books and folders in a project that has already baffled Dad. Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig and Paul Ritter star in Robert Popper's award-winning comedy

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam and Jonny finally bring their 'females' home to meet their parents, and just to add to the potential confusion both girls are called Lucy. Before the objects of their affection can make it out of the car, the boys rush into the house to give Mum and Dad a last-minute rundown of the 'rules'. Though Mum seems delighted, Dad fails to make the best first impression. Nevertheless, the evening is saved from complete disaster thanks to some exciting revelations. Comedy, starring Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig and Paul Ritter

programme.title: Frank of Ireland

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barmaid Steph Barrett is the fourth host of this week's competition in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and sets a 1940s dress code. programme.title: Gardening with Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol shows how providing nectar and pollen as forage and food for bees is not only the best way to support these vital insects, which are fast disappearing from the countryside, but also makes a glorious display in the garden. She also demonstrates how to create an auricular theatre - a traditional way of showing off collections of little plants that dates back to the 19th century, when rival enthusiasts competed in shows held in pubs in the North and Midlands.

programme.title: A Year in Kew Gardens
programme.desc: Nestled in London's suburbs is the Kew Gardens, 320 acres of the most diverse, exciting and important plant life in the world. programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: The circumstances surrounding the murder of 23-year-old Rachel Nickell in July 1992, when she was attacked and killed in front of her two-year-old son Alex on Wimbledon Common. Colin Stagg was originally charged with the murder, but was eventually acquitted in September 1994 before the case went cold. With more advanced and refined forensic techniques available, Scotland Yard later reopened the investigation and in December 2008 Robert Napper - already serving time in prison for another killing - pleaded guilty to Nickell's manslaughter

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer embarks on a Christmas tour of Norway by cruise ship, beginning her journey in Bergen, where she visits the world's largest gingerbread city. She meets a swimming Santa on a trip to Scandinavia's biggest natural aquarium, before venturing deeper into the Arctic circle to sample mead at a traditional Viking midwinter festival in a replica of a longhouse. Finally, she takes a snowmobile ride across the Russian border

programme.title: Gregg Wallace: Big Weekends Away
programme.desc: The presenter heads to Europe's oldest capital to explore a city that may have done more to shape the modern world than any other. The Greek capital Athens' 5,000 years of history are still on show and Gregg Wallace samples as much of the food, history and culture as he can in a packed three-day trip

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras focus on New Zealand's South Island, and the great Dunedin Railway's trains - the Inlander and the Seasider. The journey begins with a trip down the Taeiri Gorge towards Otago Harbour, and the area's capital, Dunedin. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Revisiting the musical highs of the decade, counting down the official top 30 biggest-selling singles in each year and looking at songs that didn't make the chart but left a lasting impression. The first edition examines 1990, with Chesney Hawkes, Shaun Ryder, Jayne Middlemiss, Paul Gambaccini, Anthea Turner, Bez and other famous faces guiding viewers through the biggest hits of the year, including Nothing Compares 2 U, Killer, The Power and U Can't Touch This

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: Memories of the year Italy hosted the World Cup, in which England lost to Germany in the semi-finals on penalties. Back home, Mrs Thatcher resigned as prime minister, reluctantly handing over the reins to John Major and Eurotunnel builders from the UK and France met 40 metres beneath the Channel, establishing the first land connection between Britain and mainland Europe for 8,000 years. Fears of the spread of BSE - so-called mad cow disease - grew, while culturally, every child wanted to be a Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle and, thanks to Madonna, everyone was Vogueing

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. He focuses on the streets of London as he reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed
