programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. In Hammersmith, west London, camera operators help police as they try to enforce a drinking ban on a local park and spot a group whose members may be about to break into a shop

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben journeys to Ireland's rugged Atlantic coastline in the winter to stay with Judith, an 80-year-old widow who rejected a modern way of life for one closer to nature. Ben learns how she and her late husband created a unique utopia and discovers how - even at an advanced age - she is determined to remain alone in the wild. Ben joins his maverick host in her solitary cottage nestled in the hillside, a treasure trove of art and books she has called home for 40 years. Shunning the modern world, Judith lives by candle-light, drinks water from a well and uses a spinning wheel

programme.title: Tattoo Disasters UK
programme.desc: People who regret their tattoos share the stories behind their body art and take steps to get them removed. A woman describes a desperate attempt to erase a former partner's name from her wrist, a man reveals how he woke from a wild night out with a slogan inked on his back and the most tattooed man in Britain reveals his latest addition. Plus, the aspiring model who now regrets getting a huge tattoo of a disgraced pop star on her stomach

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: A decision is made about a badger cub, the first baby owls of the season arrive, and a frog loses its feet

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Dr Paul Binski chronicles the development of religious architecture during the 16th and 17th centuries. He contrasts the ornate splendour of the chapel at King's College, Cambridge, with the simplicity of post-Reformation churches

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice stresses the importance of first impressions, showing a couple from Portishead, Somerset, how to spruce up the exterior of their bungalow to make it look inviting. She also offers ideas on suitable colour schemes to use inside the home, demonstrating how neutral tones can create an airy, spacious atmosphere

programme.title: The Bells of St Mary's (1945)
programme.desc: Priest Chuck O'Malley comes to the aid of a nun running a parochial school in financial difficulty. While the pair rarely see eye to eye, they pool their resources to improve conditions for the pupils. Musical drama sequel to Going My Way, with Bing Crosby reprising his Oscar-winning role alongside Ingrid Bergman, Henry Travers and Joan Carroll

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Ford's Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Anne of the Thousand Days (1969)
programme.desc: Henry VIII wins the hand of his ill-fated second wife Anne Boleyn by divorcing his first queen Katherine of Aragon, leading to a cataclysmic rift between the English Crown and the Vatican. However, when the marriage fails to produce a male heir, Henry and his council bring allegations of adultery, incest and treason against Anne, and condemn her to death. Historical drama, starring Richard Burton, Genevieve Bujold, Anthony Quayle, Michael Hordern and Irene Papas

programme.title: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader (2010)
programme.desc: Lucy and Edmund Pevensie return to the magical world accompanied by their selfish cousin. They join their old friend King Caspian as he sets sail across the sea in search of seven of his father's allies who had been banished years before, but along the way they discover an evil force is growing in strength. Fantasy adventure, with Georgie Henley, Skandar Keynes, Will Poulter and Ben Barnes

programme.title: American Made (2017)
programme.desc: A pilot is recruited to act as a courier for the CIA in the 1980s, carrying out covert deliveries from the US to General Noriega's regime in Panama. However, he exploits the opportunity to set up a profitable sideline trafficking drugs for a cartel. His dangerous double life takes a darker turn when he is ordered to supply weapons to far-right rebel groups operating in Nicaragua. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Tom Cruise and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Layer Cake (2004)
programme.desc: A London cocaine dealer is all set to leave the criminal world behind and start a new life abroad. However, his plans are thwarted when he is asked to track down a gangster's drug-addicted daughter and locate a stolen shipment of Ecstasy. Crime thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Michael Gambon, Colm Meaney, Kenneth Cranham and Sienna Miller

programme.title: Tyrannosaur (2011)
programme.desc: An alcoholic unable to control his violent temper is struck when a charity shop worker responds to his verbal abuse with forgiveness. As they become friends, he learns she is trapped in an abusive marriage and takes her in when she runs away. However, his destructive urges continue to plague him. Drama written and directed by Paddy Considine, starring Peter Mullan, Olivia Colman and Eddie Marsan

programme.title: Bugles in the Afternoon (1952)
programme.desc: A disgraced cavalryman tries to redeem himself by returning to active service at a fort in the middle of Sioux territory, unaware the commanding officer is an arch-enemy from the past. Antagonism grows as they both fall for the same woman, and the newcomer finds himself assigned to ever more dangerous missions by his commander, who hopes he will fail to return from them. Western, with Ray Milland, Helena Carter and Hugh Marlowe

programme.title: The Cassandra Crossing (1976)
programme.desc: A fleeing terrorist infects transcontinental rail passengers with a deadly virus - prompting the authorities to consider re-routing the train across a dangerously unstable bridge, hoping it will collapse and kill everyone on board to prevent the contagion spreading. Disaster epic, starring Burt Lancaster, Richard Harris, Martin Sheen and Sophia Loren

programme.title: The Tin Star (1957)
programme.desc: A world-weary bounty hunter, formerly a sheriff, arrives in a small town where a newly-appointed lawman is struggling to keep the peace. When he learns of the mercenary's past, the inexperienced lawman seeks his help in learning the tricks of the trade so he can bring an outlaw gang to justice. Anthony Mann's Western, starring Henry Fonda, Anthony Perkins, Lee Van Cleef, Betsy Palmer and Michel Ray

programme.title: Captain Scarlett (1953)
programme.desc: A brave nobleman forms a band of outlaws to free 19th-century French villagers from the tyranny of an evil duke, reclaim his birthright and save a lovely princess from a fate worse than death at the hands of a lecherous count. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Richard Greene, Leonora Amar, Nedrick Young and Manolo Fabregas

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Valkyrie (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based thriller, recreating the 1944 plot to assassinate Adolf Hitler using a briefcase bomb. As the tide of the Second World War turns in favour of the Allies, a group of senior German officers recruits war hero Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg to spearhead an attempt on the Fuehrer's life. Starring Tom Cruise, Kenneth Branagh, Bill Nighy and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Berlin Syndrome (2017)
programme.desc: After a one-night stand with a man in Berlin, an Australian photographer wakes up to discover that he has imprisoned her in his flat. Horror, starring Teresa Palmer and and Max Riemelt

programme.title: The Fighting Seabees (1944)
programme.desc: Construction workers battle against Japanese forces while working with the US navy in the South Pacific during the Second World War, while the head of the company and an officer compete for the affections of a local woman. However, when one recklessly causes a tragedy, he seeks redemption by launching an attack on a tank battalion. Drama, starring John Wayne, Susan Hayward and Dennis O'Keefe

programme.title: The Colditz Story (1954)
programme.desc: A medieval castle in Germany is turned into a PoW camp during the Second World War, and used to house the most rebellious Allied prisoners, all of whom have made repeated escape attempts. Although breaking out is supposedly impossible, the determined inmates refuse to be beaten and continue their bid for freedom. Second World War drama, starring John Mills and Eric Portman

programme.title: Arabian Nights (1942)
programme.desc: The story of two half-brothers - one the heroic caliph, the other an evil usurper - as they do battle for the right to rule their kingdom, and win the hand of beautiful dancer Sherazade. Adaptation of the classic adventure tales, starring Jon Hall, Maria Montez, Sabu and Leif Erickson

programme.title: Shane (1953)
programme.desc: A mysterious gunfighter intervenes when a man and his family are threatened by a predatory cattle baron determined to force every homesteader in the valley off the land. The situation rapidly escalates into violence, while an unspoken attraction develops between the gunman and the settler's wife. Western, starring Alan Ladd, Jack Palance, Van Heflin and Jean Arthur. Based on the novel by Jack Schaefer

programme.title: Legend (1985)
programme.desc: A peasant boy, who has a special bond with animals and fairies, shows his sweetheart the last surviving unicorns. Unfortunately, a monstrous demon and his goblin henchmen despise the mythical creatures and trick the girl into harming one of them, forcing Jack to act quickly to save her immortal soul. Ridley Scott's fantasy adventure, starring Tom Cruise, Mia Sara, Tim Curry and David Bennent

programme.title: Taken 3 (2014)
programme.desc: In the final part of the blockbusting big-screen trilogy, ex-CIA agent Bryan Mills' efforts to finally live a quiet life are shattered by the death of his ex-wife, Lenore. Just when things seemingly can't get any worse, he is framed for her murder and must fight to clear his name. Taken 2 director Olivier Megaton returns for this action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Forest Whitaker, Maggie Grace and Dougray Scott

programme.title: Raw Deal (1986)
programme.desc: An FBI agent is discharged for insubordination after assaulting a suspect, and ends up reduced to being the sheriff of a backwater Southern town. He is later offered his old job back - on the condition he will infiltrate a gang of Chicago mobsters and bring their leader to justice for killing the son of the Bureau's chief. Action thriller, with Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sam Wanamaker, Kathryn Harrold, Ed Lauter and Dare McGavin

programme.title: Paradox (2017)
programme.desc: When Hong Kong police inspector Lee Chung-chi's 16-year-old daughter disappears, he travels to Thailand in search of her, where he must deal with corrupt cops. However, he is assisted by an honest law enforcer who helps him track down culprits involved in illegal organ-trafficking. Action thriller, starring Louis Koo and Wu Yue

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Beverly signs everyone up for a holiday family photo, Geoff is excited to join in, despite Erica's reservations. Barry must face the fact that he has not been a good friend

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Feeling pressured to beef up the contents of his CV, Adam decides to try out for William Penn's pickleball team. Beverly is determined to throw Murray an epic birthday party

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When Jack and Diane have to create biographical projects about each other at school, Diane decides to shoot a documentary on Jack's budding basketball career. Andre Jr becomes a referee and takes his new responsibilities a little too far, while Zoey meets a fresh love interest on the court

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When things fall behind on the domestic front, Rainbow persuades Dre to hire a nanny. After several disappointing interviews they meet Vivian, who impresses them with her desire to take care of the family. Meanwhile, Zoey creates a smear campaign to help Diane secure the role of class president

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: To track down an infamous arms dealer, Peralta and Jeffords go undercover at an illegal gambling club, but when Captain Holt tries to coach them in the art of poker, his addiction resurfaces. Boyle buys a food truck that was formerly the site of a brutal double murder, to the horror of his only investor in the project, Santiago. Hitchcock and Scully have an unusual challenge for Dia. Cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The Vulture makes an unwelcome return to Peralta and Santiago's lives, threatening to swoop in and take something important away from them. Meanwhile, Boyle and Diaz must track down kidnapped NYPD horse Sergeant Peanut Butter, and Holt challenges Jeffords to be less image-conscious. Cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Penny agree to participate in an unusual experiment, and Leonard, Amy, Raj and Emily spend the evening trying to escape from a 'zombie'. Elsewhere, Howard and Bernadette run into trouble at the airport after returning from Mrs Wolowitz's funeral. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Trouble arises when Amy learns that Sheldon has applied for a one-way mission to colonise Mars. Meanwhile, Leonard surprises Penny with a gift, and Raj decides to snoop around Emily's flat after she is called back to work during their evening together. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Teen First Dates
programme.desc: A-level student Genevieve is desperate to make a good impression on her first-ever date, while Sonny is eager to meet a girl who shares his passion for all things 1970s

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon goes to great lengths to convince Amy that they should have a baby together after they successfully complete a scientific experiment using their DNA samples. Raj is too embarrassed to tell his friends what the new woman in his life does for a living

programme.title: World War Z (2013)
programme.desc: A virus sweeps across the globe, turning everyone infected into mindless, ravening zombies. As entire countries fall into chaos, a United Nations investigator sets off on a journey around the world to discover the cause of the pandemic and hopefully find a cure before human civilisation is brought to an end. Thriller based on the novel by Max Brooks, starring Brad Pitt and Mireille Enos

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang's preparations for Star Wars Day are interrupted by the news that Professor Proton has died. Leonard and Penny attend the funeral, where they talk about their rejected marriage proposals, but Sheldon has difficulty dealing with the death of his childhood hero - until a surprise visitor helps him cope with his grief. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco, with a guest appearance by Bob Newhart

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny gets fired from the movie, prompting her to take a serious look at her life. Raj receives advice from Sheldon after he sees Emily on a date with another man at the cinema, while Howard's mother breaks her leg. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. They then they whittle the candidates down until only one remains. In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The locked-down armchair critics share their opinions on shows including Jamie Oliver's Keep Cooking and Carry On, Celebrity Murder Mystery, Joe Wicks on The Andrew Marr Show, The Nest, This Morning with dog yoga, The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer; Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip and news coverage of the coronavirus

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The competitive nature of Taskmaster bubbles alarmingly to the surface as a simple team task involving a hammock exposes tensions between two of the five comics vying to win

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Part one of two. Joe's dying grandmother begs him to come and visit one last time on her birthday, and to bring his wife Tiffany along. He had failed to tell his family about the divorce, so Mel is forced to take a road trip to New Jersey and pose as his ex-wife at the party. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Part two of two. Joe's dying grandmother begs him to visit one last time on her birthday, and to bring his wife Tiffany along. He had failed to tell his family about the divorce, so Mel is forced to take a road trip to New Jersey to pose as his ex-wife at the party. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam offers to join Kate in adopting a vegan diet in the hope that it will bring them closer together - but finds giving up meat much harder than expected

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam throws out his back on his 50th birthday, but he is still determined to go on a dirt bike excursion despite being in pain. Meanwhile, Don and Adam give out bonuses at work

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben is still enthusiastically searching for the mysterious Elle, while Danny and Riley are hunting for a good doctor to help with the pregnancy and Bonnie desperately wants to be involved. Tucker returns from Los Angeles, but refuses to tell anyone what happened when he was there

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Tucker's mother shows up unexpectedly just as he is preparing to attend a prestigious awards ceremony with Bonnie. Riley is concerned that her pregnancy may keep her from being promoted to junior partner at the law firm

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Master sculptor and gilder Adam Williamson teaches Kirstie Allsop how to apply gold leaf to a second-hand picture frame to give it a luxury look and feel

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Phil and Caroline Mansell from Wales have been holidaying together in the La Marina Alta region of Spain's Costa Blanca for years. Phil, who suffers from severe arthritis, finds the climate helps his symptoms so they are selling their home back in the UK and are planning a full-time move. Ben Hillman shows them five properties for their £100,000 budget, but will any of them be their dream home?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dancehall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The story of how name changes in a Jewish family tested the research skills of the probate detectives, and ultimately led to an emotional family reunion. The search is also on for heirs to the six-figure estate of a talented linguist as the team attempts to trace his long-lost relatives. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two expats swap Chelmsford for Charente as they try to open their self-catering complex before the start of the summer, and Spain's newest seafood restaurant begins to make a name for itself. Meanwhile, a couple of Londoners host a traditional tea party at their new French B&B

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Five Bells near Dover, where manager Shelley Parnell and her competitive mum Linda Yates hope their bargain-priced B&B will win the day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts check in to the Thomas Paine Hotel in Thetford, where an inappropriate book is found in a bedside drawer and competition turns fierce at a Home Guard training exercise

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Chris and Sarah Whitehead invite their fellow hoteliers to Tenbury Wells in Worcestershire, where they open the doors of their historic pub The Rose and Crown

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lesley and Steve Hawker welcome the contestants to the Woodlands Guest House in Exmoor, but after the bad feedback of previous rounds, it seems their rivals are out for revenge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up to find out what they have been paid. Shelley and Linda from The Five Bells want to know why their feedback for breakfast doesn't add up, while Gez from the Thomas Paine Hotel wants to find out why Shelley and Linda scored him so low. Chris and Sarah from The Rose and Crown want to question Gez, and Steve and Lesley from Woodlands Guest House try to get to the naked truth

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The search is on for relatives of a Korean War veteran who died in London, and as the case progresses the team is able to help someone meet family members she did not know she had. Plus, the story of a Shropshire woman whose collection of assets included jewellery with a very royal connection, and details of unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Couple Mariam and Johnno arrive at their new chateau with their teenage son Monty and discover that they have no running water and that builders are busy in every room. To get respite from the heat and building work, Mariam visits Angel to get help turning her designs into tiles for her new home. Abbie and her mum Karen have wallpaper decisions to make, and wrangle oversized cots as they continue to get their chateau ready for the biggest wedding party of the season

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets architect and shed fanatic Micah Jones, who plans to transform an old agricultural building in Co Down. Micah plans an upside-down, four-bedroom shed using new techniques and materials, but the £200,000 budget runs short and Micah is forced to take on some of the building work himself

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Hampshire with Ryan and Jenny, who are hoping to bring a derelict barn back from the brink. But keeping the rickety frame upright is easier said than done, pushing project manager Ryan to the limit as he juggles his day job with running the build - and to make matters worse, self-confessed townie Jenny is not sure how she will cope in the country

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows Matt and Eloise, who leave Melbourne for Tasmania, where they are building their own home. But the tight budget means Matt must do most of the work himself - and an injury, a pregnancy and a Great Dane puppy only add to the chaos

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 58-year-old woman is brought into the hospital after being run over by a lorry, suffering severe blood loss. Doctors are concerned that nerves and tissue in her leg may have suffered irreparable damage. Meanwhile, a 65-year-old man is treated after falling from a loft ladder, and a mischievous 85-year-old shows signs of severe dehydration

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with regular team captain Sean Lock and guest captain Sarah Millican joined by Roisin Conaty and Rhod Gilbert. David O'Doherty presides alongside Countdown regular Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Hampshire with Ryan and Jenny, who are hoping to bring a derelict barn back from the brink. But keeping the rickety frame upright is easier said than done, pushing project manager Ryan to the limit as he juggles his day job with running the build - and to make matters worse, self-confessed townie Jenny is not sure how she will cope in the country

programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows Matt and Eloise, who leave Melbourne for Tasmania, where they are building their own home. But the tight budget means Matt must do most of the work himself - and an injury, a pregnancy and a Great Dane puppy only add to the chaos

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how forgotten furniture from a landfill site can be transformed, as she teams up with furniture-maker Simon Bell to revive a dining-room chair using clever painting tricks

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Creative Brighton couple Emily and Peter Harold have been holidaying on the Spanish island of Majorca for 14 years, and are determined to buy a second home there. They're looking for somewhere to relax and soak up the sun and culture on offer in their favourite spot of Port de Soller. Laura Hamilton shows them five apartments for their £250,000 budge

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is rather taken with a throne salvaged from an exotic nightclub and Simon O'Brien's restoration of a Victorian doll's house fails to go to plan

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Northamptonshire and Coventry, where Henry discovers a rare limited edition Honda Goldwing motorbike with only 65 miles on the clock

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers search for the relatives of a Northamptonshire man who left a will that was discovered to be invalid. They also visit one of the beneficiaries of a £65,000 estate, and reveal some information about his ancestor's life in India

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A blocked drain forces an expats' seafood restaurant to close at the busiest time of year, while a self-catering complex in France prepares to welcome its biggest booking yet. As two new British pub owners in Spain approach the end of the season, have they done enough to make a future in the sun?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition gets under way at Acqua Beach in Weymouth, where Charlotte Weaver and her best friend Lucy Falkingham hope their quirky design and decor makes them stand out from the crowd. But Stephen thinks the price is far from right and Derek and Lynne take exception to the decoration. During an afternoon of sand sculpting, things get personal for Adam and his partner Susan Lewes, then at dinner Derek upsets Charlotte with his comments on her design choices

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to Langmead Guest House in Ramsgate, where owners Derek and Lynne Love are all about keeping it simple. Adam and Susan are not blown away by the beige interior while Charlotte and Lucy are horrified to discover moths in the bed. During an afternoon playing crazy golf 'with a twist', simmering tension is revealed between Charlotte and Stephen, which erupts at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Big Green Bus in Lewes, where carpenter Adam Collier-Woods and his girlfriend Susan Lerski run an unconventional B&B on a converted double-decker bus. On arrival, all the guests are shocked to discover where they'll be sleeping, and during an afternoon foraging for wild garlic, professional chef Stephen plays a trick on the group as Charlotte and Lucy reveal their competitive side

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to the Pheasant hotel in Brill, Buckinghamshire, where professional chef Stephen Orr hopes his top-level place will win the day. On arrival, Adam and Susan are bowled over by the beautiful views and all the guests are surprised by the quality of the rooms. However, on closer inspection, Charlotte and Lucy are unimpressed by dust and a cobweb

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Stephen from the Pheasant tries to avoid a grilling by explaining his motives for pulling the wool over the group's eyes

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives search for the beneficiaries to an unclaimed estate worth £11,000, but they have their work cut out as the deceased has one of the most popular Welsh surnames - Jones. Plus, the hunt for the relatives of a Cheshire man reveals a family connection to the golden age of British coach travel

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant giraffe Orla goes into labour, but the keepers must watch from the sidelines due to the risks involved during the birth. Baby Bornean orang-utan Tombol tries his luck with the gibbons as he heads out and explores his new habitat. Elsewhere, northern bald ibis chick Professor X is proving too confident for his own good, and when it is time to release him into the adult habitat he gets into troubled waters and suffers a nasty head injury, so the vets have to work hard to help him pull through

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul desperately need to fill their lake with fresh water ready for the artists' retreat they are hosting. Emma steps in to model Victorian outfits for the artists, while Paul struggles on kitchen duty. Angel gives Abbie and Karen tips on how to save money for their wedding business, and she shows them how to create a stunning bar

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Chris and Kayo, who plan to build a unique, Roman-inspired home on the protected land surrounding one of Britain's most historic abbeys in Hertfordshire. The pair find themselves constrained by stringent planning conditions, and the intricate and complicated build puts both their schedule and budget under a great deal of pressure

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: When Jenny is called to a luxe condo to investigate the death of a cannabis mogul, she enters an alternate world where nothing is as it seems. She also starts to work out what it means to live life as a widow, both good and bad. Canadian crime drama, starring Serinda Swan

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 36-year-old man is rushed to St George's after being involved in a high-speed road traffic collision, with CT scans revealing a potentially life-threatening injury to his spleen. Meanwhile, a 21-year-old who dislocated his finger scoring the winning points in a basketball match believes his trip to A&E is the perfect subject for one of his vlogs

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the words-and-numbers panel game, with Sean Lock and guest captain Joe Wilkinson joined by Seann Walsh and Danny Dyer. Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner, receiving a helping hand from Bill Bailey, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Coroner

programme.title: Secret Bridesmaids' Business
programme.desc: Melanie fights for her marriage in the wake of her affair, Olivia rebounds from her failed engagement with a charming stranger, and an impetuous misstep by Saskia catches up with her. Drama, starring Abbie Cornish, Katie McGrath and Georgina Haig

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 36-year-old man is rushed to St George's after being involved in a high-speed road traffic collision, with CT scans revealing a potentially life-threatening injury to his spleen. Meanwhile, a 21-year-old who dislocated his finger scoring the winning points in a basketball match believes his trip to A&E is the perfect subject for one of his vlogs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton investigates malt, revealing how one of the unsung heroes of the kitchen cupboard is produced, while Jimmy Doherty follows the trail from Russia to Ramsgate to discover why caviar is so expensive and Matt Tebbutt examines what goes into muesli

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp creates some quick and easy 1960s-style pop art with a spray can to help transform Amber and Colin's living room

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Denise Greenacre has retired and is determined to buy a home in Spain. Ben Hillman shows Denise and her daughter Rebecca five properties around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca for her £80,000 budget

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire in search of more upcycling prospects. Simon finds some night vision goggles and an antique snooker table that's seen better days, while Henry chooses an enormous US army truck as one of his picks

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team investigates the unclaimed estate of a Nottinghamshire woman, but are unsure of its value, and another search reveals the fascinating story of working life on Britain's waterways. Plus, details of more unclaimed windfalls

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The British owners of a Spanish seafood restaurant try to lost recover revenue after a blocked drain impacted the business in a detrimental way. Plus, after a quiet summer, hopes remain that a final flurry of guests will offer a welcome boost to a fledging French B&B

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Miners Arms in Nenthead, Cumbria, where sisters Alison Baker and Lynda Clark hope that their eco credentials will impress their guests. However, brothers Andrew and James Widdas have to face their fear of shared bathrooms. The group get crafty by upcycling old rubbish, allowing Nigel and Pok U Regan to show off some hidden talents. At dinner, Kev Argent and Tor Johnson realise there's stiff competition from their fellow greenies. In the morning, Alison and Lynda face some tough feedback over facilities

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brothers Andrew and James Widdas aim to provide boutique on a budget at the Rose and Crown in Edgworth, near Bolton, but they only have eight months of experience, and B&B veterans Alison and Lynda are quick to find fault. During an afternoon of decorating gingerbread houses, Pok U and Nigel are accused of trying to bribe the hosts, and as the concept of boutique is shot down over dinner, there are simmering tensions. In the morning, Kev and Tor wonder whether anyone can challenge professional chef Andrew's breakfast spread, and Andrew and James struggle with some of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nigel and Pok U Regan cultivate an east-meets-west atmosphere at their B&B and Cantonese restaurant, the Bingham Townhouse in Nottinghamshire. Their guests aren't all happy when they arrive, with Andrew and James unsure about sleeping in single beds. Kev and Tor's focus and meditation skills are tested when the group are challenged to an archery competition. Alison and Lynda are staggered when they find out that Nigel bought the B&B on a whim, and have a bad taste in their mouths when he calls them 'sweet and sour'. At breakfast, Nigel struggles to run a smooth service

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Float by Boat near Rugby, partners Kev Argent and Tor Johnson run mindfulness breaks from their narrowboat, Spirited Away. Alison and Lynda aren't keen on the tiny shared bathroom, tight space and curtained-off bunks, and Andrew and James fret over communal living. There's tension following Nigel and Pok U's feedback, so Kev and Tor try to unite their shipmates with group meditation. At breakfast, questions are raised over the confused branding, and at feedback time all of the guests' facilities scores leave Kev and Tor speechless

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day in the competition sees Andrew and James Widdas from the Rose and Crown confront their critics about their polyester bedding. Nigel and Pok U from the Bingham Townhouse have some explaining to do when it comes to their breakfast service, while Kev and Tor from Float by Boat are left in tears by the payments they receive

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team competes against the efforts of rival probate researchers to close the case of a Hampshire man who left behind a valuable estate. Plus, the gruesome history of a barber's shop is revealed as an enormous family tree is explored

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester Zoo's penguins move into a temporary home while a new luxury home is built for them, but the move becomes upsetting when malaria sweeps the colony, with elder Rudd battling to survive against the odds. Meanwhile, the African painted dog pups venture out of their den for the first time, but the runt of the litter Mosi refuses to go out into the cold, and keepers worry she may have issues with her eyesight. Elsewhere, Sumatran orangutan Emma gives birth, and a beetle with a wonky horn has to fight other bachelors in the group to win the attention of a female

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul plan to host locals - including the mayor - to view the work they have done on the front of their chateau. However, Paul has first to design, build and, with a bit of advice from Dick Strawbridge, fit two heavy balconies from scratch. Elsewhere, Mariam and Johnno get their antique fireplace fitted, create a stylish sink out of a copper bucket, and host a 'thank you' meal for their builders

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets designers Beth Dadswell and Andrew Wilbourne, who have rescued a crumbling Victorian dairy in south-east London and plan to convert it into a family home, while retaining as much of the ramshackle roof, rust and crumbling brickwork as possible on a budget of £260,000. However, as they begin stripping back the old building, the true extent of its condition becomes plain, and the couple have to decide quite how much decay they are willing to live with

programme.title: Ice Cold Murders: Rocco Schiavone
programme.desc: Unorthodox police detective Rocco and his team initiate parallel investigations. The first involves the tragic death of an amateur rock climber while on a climb with two colleagues, and the second is triggered when a mummified corpse is discovered of a woman who died several years previously. Murder has to be considered as a possibility in both cases. Crime thriller, starring Marco Giallini. In Italian

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 64-year-old man is brought to St George's in an induced coma, having been thrown from his bicycle at high speed, with his wife by his side for support. Drama student Florian, 21, tripped and hurt his foot rushing to class, and he explains how he and his family fled war in Kosovo when he was a child, and the sacrifices his father made to get his family to safety

Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK

programme.desc: A fully relocatable polar research station in Antarctica, which was inspired by a revolutionary icebreaking ship and ingenious designs for pre-fabricated buildings

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. However, if the race did exist, it remains unclear as to why it has seemingly been eradicated from history

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces a selection of unscreened items from recent shows. programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo traces the early 20th-century roots of the Spanish Civil War, which divided his family and sent his father into exile. The former politician discovers a nation fractured at the time by social tensions and regional loyalties, which today offer a rich diversity of cultures to delight the tourist. After arriving in Majorca from Barcelona, Michael spoils himself enjoying spectacular scenic views aboard a 1912 vintage railway and a 1913 tram. He also gets trampled underfoot at the bottom of a Catalan 'people steeple' and learns to make the perfect paella

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a Bradford-built 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Paul and Dave uncover a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: On a wartime bomber base in Lincolnshire, veteran engineer Gerry Cooper rebuilds two pre-war biplanes with glorious histories; the US Navy N3N and the Morane Saulnier 315

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: The impact of an integrated defence system and radar on the Second World War, focusing on the role of RAF Kenley, one of the most intact Battle of Britain airfields

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Japan would create arguably the greatest fighter in the Second World War - the Mitsubishi Zero, but their failed War Factories would ultimately reduce the Zero to a kamikaze plane

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Lancaster factory, one of the biggest buildings in Europe at the time, helped to create a truly war-winning weapon

programme.title: The Stuarts: A Bloody Reign
programme.desc: Professor Kate Williams examines the Wynn family's return to favour during the Reformation, and the key events of Charles II's reign, including the Great Fire of London

programme.desc: Professor Kate Williams examines the final downfall of the Wynn family during the brief reign of James II before his overthrow by his daughter and her husband

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Titanic: Stories from the Deep
programme.desc: Thanks to the use of cutting-edge technology, a unique collection of artefacts salvaged from the underwater resting site of the famous wreck suggest new stories of love, deception, fate and heroics. This first episode touches on Fifth Officer Harold Lowe. After Titanic sank, his lifeboat was one of the few to go back and pick up survivors. One of the people he saved, Renee Harris, gifted him a set of gold-plated nautical equipment as a symbol of her gratitude. Plus, a well-preserved alligator bag recovered from the wreck site containing fascinating clues about the life of its owner

programme.title: Titanic: Stories from the Deep
programme.desc: Exploring the high cost of surviving the sinking, including the tragic story of Frederick Fleet, one of two lookouts, whose quick actions may have averted an even great disaster

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Jerusalem in search of Solomon's Temple. When the Romans sacked the city in 70AD, the fabled Treasure of Solomons was believed to have been looted and taken back to Rome. However, it is possible it was hidden away by the High Priests. Archaeologists Dr Shimon Gibson and Kate Raphael have dedicated their lives working out what actually happened, and discuss whether it is possible that Solomonic and Roman treasures lie undiscovered under Jerusalem

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme embarks on its 40th anniversary tour by paying a visit to the stately home of Castle Howard, near York. Host Fiona Bruce is joined by the programmes first presenter, Bruce Parker, who helps to assess the secret of the Roadshow's success

Andi Oliver has the meaty task of learning how doner kebabs are made and bags herself an invite to the fancy award ceremony known as the Kebaftas

programme.title: How to Save a Grand in 24 Hours
programme.desc: Anna Richardson and the team delivers financial home truths to a self-employed couple from Portsmouth, who have found themselves reduced to a single income following the birth of their second child. Eve Humphreys provides some low-cost DIY tips, while Gary Usher shows them how to make their favourite takeaway at home for a fraction of the cost

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The stories of children and teenagers brought into A&E. Sixteen-year-old Ellie has suspected spinal injuries after being thrown from her horse and crushed against a fence, while consultant Jacqui Butler treats four-year-old Destiny, who has a fishbone stuck in his tonsils from his lunchtime curry. Plus, a pregnant woman experiences intense pain following a sudden haemorrhage

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to an emergency where a three-month-old baby has stopped breathing and is unconscious. The paramedics attend a road traffic collision involving a quad biker. In Bromsgrove, the fall of an elderly woman becomes life threatening as paramedics suspect she is suffering a stroke and they need to get her to hospital fast

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Davina McCall: Sex, Myths and the Menopause
programme.desc: Davina McCall was 44 when she began to experience hot flushes, depression and confusion - symptoms she hid until she discovered her body was starting the transition to menopause. Here she describes her own experience to bust the myths surrounding the menopause, tackling the mid-life taboos that can destroy women's jobs and relationships, and exposing the lack of specialist education for GPs and the confusion that still exists around hormone replacement therapy

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Home cook Lulu Marodeen brings tinned salmon to take on Mark Sargeant. A battle of the bananas commences, as Michelin-starred chef Mark turns out a fruity tarte Tatin with ginger biscuits. Will Lulu be tasting sweet success with the jury or will soggy pastry leave her chances of £10,000 in tatters? Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Beach House in Portland, Dorset, where Pete Lacey feels his back-to-basics approach offers his guests good value. However, things get heated with the radiator in Karen and Liam's room and opinions are laid bare over dinner

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Married couple Lynne and Ian kick off the competition in Gateshead, Tyne and Wear, with a menu of hearty treats. On day two newly-weds Jess and Connor cook up a Greek meal - including a rather large mezze - followed by a Greek dance and some plate smashing. After two high-energy dinner parties, engaged couple Tom and Sam's chances rest on their banana souffle

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Kathryn and Mark find a home on the Kassandra Peninsula in the north of Greece, with plenty of room for the family and near to the beach on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Reg and Aby Solomon from Hertfordshire look to buy a family holiday home near the Spanish town of Denia on the popular Costa Blanca. Laura Hamilton searches every nook and cranny of the local market in an attempt to find properties that match the couple's £200,000 budget, but it remains to be seen whether she can deliver the perfect place in the perfect location

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Gareth Sadd love the northern part of Tenerife and are keen to find a bolt-hole that they can escape to regularly. Presenter Jasmine Harman searches the local market to find properties to match the couple's £110,000 budget in the hope that they'll like one of them enough to make an offer

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shop manager Nicola Steele hosts the first dinner party in Cardiff, and hopes that her dog-themed surprises and unusual entertainment will impress her guests - army wife Rachael Smith, casting agency owner Jerry Lockett, childminder Andrea Grainger and chatterbox Sam Clark - and win her the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jerry Lockett hosts the second dinner party in Cardiff, and following a flood at his home, hopes to impress his guests by serving the meal at the grand venue of his cousin's mansion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Army wife Rachael Smith hosts the third party in Cardiff, allowing her competitive edge and no-nonsense approach to come to the fore in her bid to pick up the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Costume designer Sam Clarke prepares a Mexican menu for the fourth dinner party in Cardiff. Everything does not go to plan, however, when two guests begin fighting at the table, and his 'fun' game leaves everyone in tears

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Childminder Andrea Grainger hosts the last dinner party as the competition in Cardiff draws to a close. She hopes a menu of traditional Welsh dishes will help her secure the prize, and lines up a selection of games to improve her chances of success. As the evening comes to an end, the diners discover which of the meals made the best impression

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie prepares to end the year in style by throwing a Christmas dinner to remember. However, with the ancient range cooker out of action and the 19th-century bread oven put into service cooking the turkey, it's a tall order to deliver a five-course extravaganza for 17 guests. Angel sets Dick the task of finding and chopping the perfect logs for her Christmas table display in their 12-acre grounds. Christmas cheer threatens to run dry for south Londoners Lee and Belinda as freezing temperatures and a broken boiler put their first ever festive event in peril

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Steve and Aniko want to escape the rat race and move to the peace and quiet of the French countryside in west Brittany. Steve is a music composer and Aniko a singer, so their dream would be to be able to create their own recording studio at home. With a budget of £90,000, property expert Jonnie Irwin lines up five options that he hopes could be just the ticket for the pair

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
programme.desc: The experts focus on wild gardens that celebrate nature. Poppy Okotcha creates a wild planting design that requires little maintenance, Joel Bird demonstrates how to make a living tipi, and Bruce Kenneth builds a table with its own herb patch and built-in bar. The team also visits a family in the Pennine hills looking for an outside space that goes with their view of the Saddleworth countryside

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in south London. Elliot and James want to buy their first place together but are struggling to pin down exactly where. Their favourite areas are Putney and Richmond, but their money does not go as far as they would like. Pam and Ross are in Wimbledon, where former tennis professional Ross works. However, having been stung after the 2009 housing crisis, they are understandably cautious

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to an emergency where a three-month-old baby has stopped breathing and is unconscious. The paramedics attend a road traffic collision involving a quad biker. In Bromsgrove, the fall of an elderly woman becomes life threatening as paramedics suspect she is suffering a stroke and they need to get her to hospital fast

programme.title: Den of Thieves (2018)
programme.desc: An elite unit of the Los Angeles county sheriffs' department investigates a series of perfectly executed heists, and uncovers a criminal gang composed of military veterans, whose strategic and combat experience make them an elusive foe. The investigators set out to bring them to justice before they can carry out their biggest robbery yet - stealing £120million from the Federal Reserve Bank. Director Christian Gudegast's crime drama, starring Gerard Butler and Jordan Bridges

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The 21 new recruits for the sixth series face an intense start when the train they are travelling on is the subject of a fake hijack to test the passengers' reactions. The trainees are then pitted them against each other in a race up a 4000ft mountain, followed by a 100ft abseil off an oil rig, as the team of instructors look for people who can handle anything that is thrown at them

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes 24-year-old Sian from Norfolk, a petrol head who wants to find a man she can love more than her beloved car, and 33-year-old fashion entrepreneur Tansel from London. He is looking to settle down and hopes that losing ignoring the clothes for once will help him find the right girl

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The penultimate episode sees Sarah Kendall, Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack and Mike Wozniak all still in with a chance of winning Greg Davies's splendid gilded head. Lee has a makeover, Jamali gets destructive with some rubble, and Mike warns Greg's assistant Alex Horne about some potentially dangerous dairy produce. Meanwhile, Sarah is still the one to catch

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Countdown's Nick Hewer joins singer and TV personality Stacey Solomon, the Kaiser Chiefs' Ricky Wilson and Diversity dancer Perri Kiely for the baking challenge. Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted tasks, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their skills. On the menu are scones, a devilish technical, and a showstopper challenge in the form of self-portrait croquembouches. Which of the famous faces will impress the judges?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out how much they've been paid and to settle some scores. Karen and Liam are shocked by their payment from Pete, while Carolynne and George want to find out what caused Karen and Liam to lose sleep at their place

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, three couples compete to host the best dinner party. Sarah and Paddy kick things off with a fine-dining feast for guests Louise and Danny and Emma and Samson. The hosts mix Eastern and traditional fare. Then, on night two Emma and Samson treat their guests to some Mozambican delights. After a first course that whistles and wows, palates need to be cleansed for the main course, but a chilly chat about ice cream leaves Louise red in the face. On the final night, Louise and Danny confound everyone's expectations with a sailing-based starter. A spot of model behaviour excites the guests, and a mountain of meringue impresses, but is it enough to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired bus driver Sue and postal worker Ali are relocating from Weston-super-Mare to inland Spain and hope Ben Hillman can find them a permanent home on their budget of £70,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Liverpool couple John and Pauline McDonough both have demanding jobs in the pharmaceutical industry and want to find a relaxing bolt-hole to escape to in Spain's Granada province. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties starting at just under £86,000 in the hope that they will make an offer

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps married couple Ali and Ben Brown from Kent find their ideal property abroad, as she shows them five places on the Spanish island of Gran Canaria

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Newcastle is hosted by mother and workaholic Clare, who aims to impress her guests with an Indian-themed menu of spicy pakoras and lamb curry in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedy promoter David hosts the second night in Newcastle, where he aims to impress his guests with a fine-dining menu featuring caviar, scallops, beef fillet and oysters, in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize. However, some of the guests have reservations about the host, meaning he has his work cut out for him

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third night in Newcastle sees sophisticated marketing consultant Liz trying to impress her guests with a fancy French-themed menu. However, she spends most of her time trying to keep the peace as outspoken comedy promoter David annoys club singer John and rubs mum-of-three Clare up the wrong way with his snobbish views

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Club singer John hopes that a menu inspired by his travels around the world will help him win the £1,000 prize as he hosts the fourth night in Newcastle. He also has a musical surprise in store for his guests, but faces a challenge trying to impress David who refused to take part in the fun and games on Liz's evening. However, further rifts appear in the group when Vicky wants to discuss Geordie icons

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Newcastle and the turn of hotel administrator Vicky to entertain her guests. However, there is tension in the air and the spiritual host is challenged to keep the peace when John lets David know exactly what he thinks of him. The theme of negativity continues as mum-of-three Claire and Liz both take the comedy promoter to task over his questionable sense of humour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is revealed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A recent storm leaves Lee and Belinda's castle keep in need of urgent repairs - and Lee must face his fear of heights if he is to repair the hole in the roof

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shane and Sharon dream of owning a holiday home on the Costa del Sol that they can escape to during the winter to enjoy the area's all-year round outdoor lifestyle. With a budget of £135,000, they want an apartment or town house close to the beach around La Cala de Mijas, with two bedrooms so their family can come to visit. Danni Menzies rises to the challenge to find them the perfect property

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect travels to Macclesfield to meet a first-time builder who hopes to transform an allotment shed into a Doctor Who-esque, multi-functional play den for less than £300. He also visits a spectacular live-in tree house in Scotland, and creates a medieval-style lock for the wood cabin, with Will's help. The Italian road trip takes in a holiday lodge made almost entirely from glass in the Dolomites

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect visits Cumbrian farmer Adam, who is turning a 1970s Bedford fire truck into a mobile shop. Will Hardie travels to Wales to meet Joelle, a teacher transforming a dilapidated pony trailer into a holiday retreat on a budget of just £1,000, while on the Italian road trip, George views a tiny house perched on top of a cliff. The wilderness cabin is also revealed in all its glory

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in south London. Elliot and James want to buy their first place together but are struggling to pin down exactly where. Their favourite areas are Putney and Richmond, but their money does not go as far as they would like. Pam and Ross are in Wimbledon, where former tennis professional Ross works. However, having been stung after the 2009 housing crisis, they are understandably cautious

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: As the UK enters its fourth week of lockdown, West Midlands Ambulance Service comes to the aid of the staff of care homes, who are feeling forgotten as the virus claims the lives of so many residents. It falls on crews like Julie and Adam to provide help and reassurance. Back in the control centre, Irum has had fast-track training and is plunged into the stark realities of her new role, while a national minute's silence for NHS and care workers holds a particular significance for her

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted tasks, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their skills. On the menu are scones, a devilish technical, and a showstopper challenge in the form of self-portrait croquembouches. Which of the famous faces will impress the judges?

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: Frank doesn't react well when he learns that Mary is planning to sell their home, meaning he'll finally have to move out. The second she leaves the house, he locks her out and claims ownership, barricading himself in - along with Aine, Doofus, Nicola and Stephane - and refusing to give up his territory. However, with Mary equally determined to get back in the property, the two face-off in a battle of wills. Comedy, starring Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: More privileged young Brits discover the harsh realities of living on the breadline. In the first edition, the son of a property tycoon moves in with a Preston family

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: The opening night of Dave and Tony's Mexican restaurant looks like being a washout when the rain pours down - and the weather doesn't exactly help mother-and-daughter bar owners Lisa and Tia when they advertise a Friday night barbecue. Dog rescuer Linda is worried about sick pup Donny, while Sheffield retirees Phil and Jackie - known for their colourful, matching outfits - scour the charity shops for more crazy clothing bargains

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding is fast approaching, and rail staff have their work cut out to get the station ready for the arrival of thousands of fans hoping to catch a glimpse of the couple. Slough station manager Alison is preparing for the big day - and from crowd control barriers to decorative bunting, there is plenty to be done

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: A decade of health problems has resulted in Elaine's two-bedroom flat in Rochester, Kent, being in dire need of a deep clean, but cat sick on the floor and an infestation of fleas make it one of the toughest jobs that Jon and Brennan have ever taken on

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire officers are called out to a massive crash near a theme park that has resulted in the death of at least one person. The need to understand what has happened and who - if anyone - is to blame tests the resources of all the traffic cops who attend and then investigate this fatal incident. Will their gut instincts that this is a drug-fuelled disaster prove correct?

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents an edition focusing on the hunt for disqualified, unlicensed and uninsured drivers. In Bradford, officers from the West Yorkshire Safer Roads team pursue a desperate and disqualified driver through the city streets, while a member of the public is forced into making a citizen's arrest when another banned motorist attempts to flee the scene of a crash. In York, a pensioner is knocked down in a hit-and-run incident and an urgent search for the culprit gets under way

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mark gets lucky when a car thief crosses paths with him, there is a high-speed pursuit through Lincoln city centre, and a member of the public helps the interceptors track down a drink-driver. Elsewhere, Jon Peacock gets an unusual request and Jon Moon has an encounter with a surprising lawbreaker

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team carries out a raid that turns up a stash of drugs, a weapon and a large pile of cash, while Jon Peacock is amazed to encounter a half-naked man covered in blood on the wintry streets of Lincoln, but the real surprise comes when the officer finds out what he has been up to. Suspicious driving leads Jim Woolerton to discover super-strength lager and drugs, and a speeding motorist comes out with a very middle-class excuse after being clocked travelling at 110mph

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan Brigginshaw tracks down a driver who has given him the slip on the side roads of Spalding, while Suzie Ranyard comes to the rescue of a motorist with a rather large passenger. Jim Woolerton utilises some of the unit's most advanced kit to catch out a disqualified driver and officers are called out when a dead body is spotted in one of Lincolnshire's many waterways

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Doctors treat an elderly patient rushed into hospital suffering from a racing heartbeat, and a 28-year-old man receives treatment after a motorbike accident. Nurse Richard Jackson meets Graham, a patient with a complex medical history, who has been brought in with the symptoms of a stroke

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Aaron and David are shocked to hear Emmett is responsible for the robbery. David pushes Brent into not letting Emmet take the fall

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Bella and Nikau head off to a cosy weekend retreat, but Chloe and Ryder show up to ruin their private time. John still battles guilt, and Justin needs stronger pain medication

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Interviews with the people whose holidays ended in disaster. Highlights include Jody and Stacey Mortlock, whose dream honeymoon turned into a nightmare when the Covid-19 pandemic started. Their tour operator abandoned them in Egypt. As their hotel closed, they had to make a dash to the airport to get out of the country. Plus, the experiences of the Evans family, who booked a dream break in Cuba - but on arrival found smelly rooms and swimming pools doubling as toilets. With contributions by travel experts Danni Menzies, Scarlette Douglas, Jean Johansson and Simon Calder. Narrated by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Bargain Brits on Benefits
programme.desc: Cameras follow bulk buyer Zana, who shops for the biggest offers, and Allan, who has invested in a yacht to live a champagne lifestyle on a lemonade budget

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: The monumental task of cleaning the home of Andrew in Norfolk, who has filled his cluttered home with toys, models and computers. There are cat faeces throughout, and an infestation of bugs in the kitchen

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: The team helps a 63-year-old woman whose flat is packed with junk that she can't bear to throw away, while house clearance expert George Mensah is called in by a bereaved family to help with the task of dealing with a lifetime of hoarding

programme.title: White House Down (2013)
programme.desc: A policeman is turned down for his dream job as a secret service bodyguard to the president. While taking his daughter on a tour of the White House, he gets the chance to prove himself when terrorists seize control of the building, putting the head of state's life in his hands. Action thriller, with Channing Tatum, Jamie Foxx, Maggie Gyllenhaal and James Woods

Action thriller, with Channing Tatum, Jamie Foxx, Maggie Gyllenhaal and James Woods

programme.title: Rich Holiday, Poor Holiday
programme.desc: Wealthy Cheshire entrepreneur Jude Cisse takes her family to experience a caravan break in Devon, and has trouble adjusting to the lack of privacy their crammed campsite provides. The hard-up McQueens from Peckham, who survive on dad Jay's £450 a week bus-driver wage, last went on holiday two years ago in a caravan on the South Coast. They take a trip to Jude's private, staffed villa in Morocco and are initially awe-struck at the luxury they experience

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits in Blackpool
programme.desc: Holidaymakers Mick and Joanne from the Midlands fall for Scarborough's charms on their first visit, so much so they begin considering making a permanent move. In Skegness, John Nuttall, his ex-wife Michelle and their 14-year-old daughter Thalia welcome a new addition to their donkey family, while a heatwave in Blackpool means a chance for self-made millionaire Howard to maximise his car-parking profits

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Australian soap opera, charting the lives and loves of the residents of the suburb of Erinsborough
